label: Islamic Authority Sources: Qur'an, Hadith, Tafsir

source.title: Understanding Islamic Textual Authority
source.summary: Islamic religious authority comes from three main sources: (1) QUR'AN - The primary scripture, believed to be Allah's direct revelation to Muhammad through angel Gabriel. Organized into 114 surahs (chapters). Considered infallible and eternal. (2) HADITH - Collections of Muhammad's sayings, actions, and approvals, transmitted through chains of narrators. The most authentic collections are Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. Used to interpret and apply the Qur'an. (3) TAFSIR - Scholarly commentaries on the Qur'an, explaining context, interpretation, and application. Ibn Kathir's tafsir is widely respected. Unlike the Qur'an, tafsir is human scholarship and can be questioned. Additionally, IJMA (scholarly consensus) and QIYAS (analogical reasoning) help derive rulings. Understanding this hierarchy helps Christians navigate conversations about authority and scripture.
